edges.ev: COMT essentially contributes to the formation of S units from 5-OH-G precursors
reduction in CCoAOMT could effectively affect the availability of the substrate feruloyl CoA
repression of CCoAOMT results in a decrease of both guaiacyl lignin and syringyl lignin units
repression of CCoAOMT results in a decrease of both guaiacyl lignin and syringyl lignin units
CCR catalyzes the conversion of feruloyl-CoA to coniferaldehyde in monolignol biosynthesis
S/G ratio of FAS13 and FS40 lignin was significantly lower than that of wild type; relatively more reduced in syringyl than in guaiacyl units
Ferulic acid was incorporated into the lignin via ether bonds; reduces relative S-unit incorporation
CAD catalyzes the last step in the biosynthesis of the lignin precursors, the monolignols
CAD reduces coniferyl and sinapyl aldehydes to their respective alcohols
C3'H is a cytochrome P450 that catalyzes the 3'-hydroxylation of p-coumaroyl shikimate
increase in p-hydroxyphenyl monomers came at the expense of only guaiacyl (G)
C3H downregulation reduced lignin levels by half; G/S: 0.59 WT to 0.28 C3H (S/G decreased); H increased ~100-fold
the S/G ratio in DEL-CK was 2.82, while the S/G ratio for DEL-C3H was 2.48
4CL catalyzes activation of 4-coumarate to 4-coumaroyl-CoA
reductions in S/G ratios as expected, by thioacidolytic analysis; S/G declined to 1.1-1.4 in event 712 vs 1.8-2.3 control
F5H1 and F5H2 significantly downregulated in 4CL1-KO (CRISPR); S/G decreased 20-30%
pyMBMS analysis detected 30% decrease in S/G ratio; 34% reduction of S-units and 5% reduction of G-units in 4CL1-KO
Upregulation of CCoAOMT1 drives caffeate-to-caffeoyl flux toward G-lignin in 4CL1-KO background
CSE is able to catalyze the conversion of caffeoyl shikimate into caffeate
S/G ratio decreased from 2.06 (wild-type) to 1.85 and 1.90 in hpCSE#1 and hpCSE#2 lines
HCT directs caffeoyl-shikimate formation; xylem-specific expression in PtrHCT1 and PtrHCT6
PAL catalyzes the initial step of phenylpropanoid biosynthesis; deaminates phenylalanine to cinnamate
C4H hydroxylates cinnamate to p-coumarate in phenylpropanoid pathway
CCoAOMT methylates caffeoyl-CoA to feruloyl-CoA
CCR converts feruloyl-CoA to coniferaldehyde
CAD reduces coniferaldehyde to coniferyl alcohol
5-hydroxyconiferyl aldehyde was converted exclusively into sinapyl aldehyde when incubated with LsCOMT
CAD reduces sinapaldehyde to sinapyl alcohol
sinapyl alcohol is the S monolignol; high sinapyl alcohol gives high S/G
coniferyl alcohol is the G monolignol; excess coniferyl alcohol decreases S/G
4CL5 compensatory pathway channeled toward G-lignin; 4CL5 activates upon 4CL1 loss
CCR2 null + haploinsufficient allele shifts lignin composition; S units reduced in CCR-suppressed poplars
transcript levels of two distinct genes encoding Phe ammonia lyase (PAL) were elevated in CCR-downregulated poplars
HCT converts p-coumaroyl-CoA to caffeoyl shikimate; required for G and S lignin biosynthesis

perturbations.ev: 70SOMT-3: 10-fold reduction in beta-O-4-linked S units (60% to 6%); almost complete lack of syringyl units
ASOMT lines: 65-67% G vs 29-31% S (vs 33-34% G; 64-67% S control)
70% reduction in CCoAOMT activity; lignin reduced to 60% wildtype; both G and S reduced proportionally so S/G largely maintained
CCR transcript levels down to 3-4% of wild-type; S/G ratio of FAS13 and FS40 lignin significantly lower than wild type; relatively more reduced in S than G units
Null CCR2 allele combined with haploinsufficient allele alters lignin composition without yield penalty; S units reduced
Neither the lignin amount nor the lignin monomeric composition (syringyl/guaiacyl) were significantly modified
C3'H RNAi: 20.6% H units, 60% S, 19.3% G; increase in H at expense of G only; S maintained
C3H downregulation: G/S 0.59 WT to 0.28 C3H; H increased ~100-fold to 31%
S/G in DEL-CK was 2.82 vs 2.48 for DEL-C3H; lignin content 25.94 to 20.52%
4CL antisense: S/G ratios declined to 1.1-1.4 in event 712 vs 1.8-2.3 control
4CL1 CRISPR-KO: 20-30% decrease in S/G; 34% S-unit reduction and 5% G-unit reduction
CRISPR-KO of 4CL5 did not change either lignin content or S/G ratio
S/G ratio decreased from 2.06 WT to 1.85 and 1.90 in hpCSE#1 and hpCSE#2; S decreased by 3-4%
C3H downregulation reduced S/G from 2.82 to 2.48 and lignin content 25.94% to 20.52%
